name: Beijing
intro: Beijing is the strongest first-stop city for travelers who want imperial landmarks, museums, hutong neighborhoods, and straightforward high-speed rail connections.
seoTitle: Beijing Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Beijing with practical advice on neighborhoods, timing, local transport, and flagship sights.
tripPace: Best with a structured city plan and one slower cultural day.
highlights: A strong first-stop city if the traveler wants headline China landmarks and a sense of imperial history.
District-based planning works especially well because the city is large and major sights are not all close together.
Pairs naturally with another city that offers a different rhythm, such as Xi'an or Shanghai.

planningPriorities.title: Decide whether Beijing is the anchor city
planningPriorities.description: Use Beijing as the main historical stop, not as a city where every day is split between opposite ends of town.

planningPriorities.title: Choose a hotel area before polishing the itinerary
planningPriorities.description: A convenient base can reduce transfers, simplify early departures, and make evenings less tiring.

planningPriorities.title: Group major sights by geography
planningPriorities.description: Plan around central landmarks, one Great Wall day, and one slower local neighborhood day instead of mixing everything together.

transportNote: Beijing rewards travelers who plan by district and metro access because cross-city travel can take longer than the map suggests.
